summary: Drive built-in skill/rule extraction through the embedded KIND definitions and the generic composed path; retire the hand-coded `skill_features`/`rule_features`

per.section: The extraction algebra â€” the soundness boundary, as data

edit.description: Add the adapter read face: turn a `Skill`/`Rule` IR into a `crate::kind::Unit` (frontmatter from the typed fields + `extra`, the byte-faithful body, provenance, `satisfies`, `published_requirements`) and run the embedded kind's `Extraction::extract` to yield `extract::Features`. This IRâ†’Unit conversion is the engine-code adapter the source-not-mechanism direction leaves sanctioned; the per-field feature mapping moves into the composed `kinds/*/KIND.md` extraction.

Retire `skill_features` and `rule_features` (the hand-coded per-field mappings) and their `#[cfg(test)]` unit tests â€” symbol retirement as an EDIT, never a `retire` path (inbox fence correction). Keep the shared `pub(crate)` surface extractors (`body_line_count`/`body_headings`/`body_sections`/`source_dir_name`/`json_to_feature`) the composed path already reuses; the `Features`/`FeatureValue`/`Kind`/`Section` types stay.

Repoint the built-in feature call sites â€” `run_check` (~541â€“545) and the `Why` command (~405â€“408) â€” from `extract::skill_features`/`extract::rule_features` to the embedded skill/rule KIND extraction driver in `builtin_kind`. `assemble_by_kind`, the `engine::validate` streams, and coverage/graph tiers are otherwise unchanged (they range over `Features`).

Repoint the snapshots from `skill_features`/`rule_features` to the new embedded-extraction driver; the committed `.snap` files MUST remain byte-identical â€” the unchanged snapshot is the equivalence proof.

tests.asserts: the embedded-extraction driver produces `Features` byte-identical to the pinned `skill_features`/`rule_features` snapshots (the `.snap` files are unchanged).

acceptance: built-in skill/rule `check` features are computed by running the embedded `kinds/*/KIND.md` extraction over an IR-derived `Unit`; `skill_features`/`rule_features` are gone; the pinned equivalence snapshot is unchanged; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test and the self-check gate all green.
notes: Completes the wave. `skill_features`/`rule_features` (hand-coded field mapping) retire as an EDIT to src/extract.rs naming the symbols, never a `retire` path (inbox fence correction). Engine code stays only at adapter faces (Skill/Rule IRâ†’Unit; parse/emit). Equivalence proven by the unchanged pin snapshot; blocked serially so no open entry shares these files.

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind â€” `.claude/agents/*.md` imported as one AGENT.md member document, checked by an authored agent package

gate.reason: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction under the requirements/satisfies reframe; the surface-language chain it waited on has shipped, so the blocker is priority alone â€” revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The Agent IR â€” the typed model for a Claude Code subagent at `.claude/agents/<name>.md`, mirroring `src/rule.rs`: typed YAML frontmatter on the projection side (`name`, `description`, optional `tools`/`model`), unknown keys preserved, content-faithful body, provenance. Identity is the path (file stem). The surface member is one AGENT.md document (clause-module header) like every kind.

The agent kind's authored built-in package (clauses + guidance) in the one-document medium, embedded with the std-lib by build.rs (the packages/ product tree â€” NOT a contracts/agent.toml). Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]`; `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores; an advisory `max_lines`.
Fixture-driven proof mirroring `tests/rule_contract.rs`: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an insta import round-trip proving idempotence.

edit.description: Add `pub mod agent;` beside the other artifact-kind modules (this entry owns the line).
Extend `Workspace` with `agents` loaded under `<dir>/agents/*` and a `WorkspaceError` arm; update the load tests.
Add `agent_features` mirroring the built-in extraction driver (post-unification this is the embedded KIND path, not a hand-coded feature fn).
Discover `.claude/agents/*.md`, project each to one `<into>/agents/<name>/AGENT.md` member document, add an `[[agent]]` lock row per agent.
Bind the agent package, wire admissibility + conformance streams and `by_kind` for the kind.

tests.asserts: each decidable clause of the agent package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the agent package (conformance) and that package passes admissibility; `temper import` projects agents as one-document members idempotently with `[[agent]]` lock rows; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Deferred â€” more built-in kinds is the wrong direction under the requirements/satisfies reframe; revive only if a story demands it. On revival, add the agent KIND definition under kinds/ (post-unification) and serialize its shared-file blast radius (lib/check/extract/import/main) via blockedBy against any then-open entry touching those files.

summary: Ship acquisition channels â€” npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install â€” auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected. Distinct from the existing `.github/workflows/temper.yml` CI job.

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`, confirmed on disk). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's pattern) plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/50-distribution.md`). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than clobbering the harness manifest. Scoped/prefixed names per the resolved (project-name) fork.

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infra, mostly outside `src/`; the cargo gates can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human creds (npm org, tap, signing). NB: root `package.json` already exists as the *private* flume manifest â€” reconcile or split, never blindly create a second. Parked until a human sets creds up.

summary: Add CONTRIBUTING.md and SECURITY.md â€” the two missing launch community docs (two-sided AI-authorship disclosure; vuln-reporting evidence bar)

per.section: The community surface â€” small, honest, gated

gate.reason: build's writable fence (.flume/chain.ts BUILD_WRITABLE_PATHS) lists only README/CHANGELOG/AGENTS/LICENSE among root docs â€” no root CONTRIBUTING.md/SECURITY.md glob â€” so build would bail; a human must widen the fence (chain.ts is human territory) and enable private vulnerability reporting (a repo setting) before build can author these

new.description: Small and honest per the Decision: how to file a good issue, 'search first', PRs by prior discussion, and the two-sided AI-authorship policy (inbound AI-assisted contributions welcome WITH disclosure; the contributor must understand and defend the change without the assistant). Expectation-setting, not an invitation to co-maintain.

Vulnerability-reporting policy with an explicit evidence bar â€” a report must demonstrate, not speculate (the 'unverified AI-generated vuln reports are the new spam' lesson). Points at private vulnerability reporting (the GitHub repo setting a human enables).

acceptance: CONTRIBUTING.md and SECURITY.md exist at root, matching specs/55's launch-set Decisions (two-sided disclosure; demonstrate-not-speculate); the fence has been widened so build's entry-fence preflight admits them.
notes: Launch set (specs/55). Already on disk: README, AGENTS.md, CHANGELOG.md, LICENSE-MIT/APACHE, issue forms (.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/{bug,feature,config}.yml). Missing only CONTRIBUTING.md + SECURITY.md. Both are root-level per the spec but outside build's root-docs fence â€” parked on a human fence-widen (chain.ts) + enabling private reporting. Parked entries are skipped by the entry-fence gate, so this files cleanly.
